_about: Curated subset of MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise techniques most relevant to AI-agent attack scenarios. Source: https://attack.mitre.org/ — public, vendor-neutral framework. Used by demo_seed_v2.py to tag every demo event with a real T-ID so customers see the framework they already know.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries use credentials of existing accounts to gain Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.
techniques.agent_relevance: Stolen agent passport tokens; agent acting outside its sanctioned scope.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries obtain and abuse credentials of cloud accounts to gain access to cloud resources.
techniques.agent_relevance: Compromised service-account credentials used by an autonomous agent.

techniques.name: Brute Force: Credential Stuffing
techniques.description: Adversaries try lists of stolen credentials at high velocity hoping for matches.
techniques.agent_relevance: Bot-driven credential testing against agent identity endpoints.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries manipulate accounts to maintain or elevate access to victim systems.
techniques.agent_relevance: Agent self-modifying its own permission scope (RSA gap 1).

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.description: Adversaries circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevated privileges.
techniques.agent_relevance: Agent escalating tool-call access mode without operator approval.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries use valid accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections.
techniques.agent_relevance: Cross-org agent action without federation handshake.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
techniques.description: Adversaries authenticate using stolen tokens or session cookies.
techniques.agent_relevance: Replay of attestation bundle from a compromised agent.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts
techniques.description: Adversaries establish persistence by modifying scripts executed during system startup.

techniques.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
techniques.description: Adversaries steal session cookies to bypass MFA.

techniques.name: Steal Application Access Token
techniques.agent_relevance: OAuth tokens issued to agents being exfiltrated.

techniques.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
techniques.description: Adversaries transfer data to attacker-controlled cloud storage.
techniques.agent_relevance: Agent invokes exfil-class MCP tool against attacker URL.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries steal data by exfiltrating over a non-C2 protocol.
techniques.agent_relevance: MCP send_email / http_request after a sensitive read — classic chain pattern.

techniques.name: Data Staged
techniques.description: Adversaries stage data to a central location before exfiltration.
techniques.agent_relevance: Bulk read followed by write — MCP data_staging chain pattern.

techniques.name: Create Account
techniques.description: Adversaries create accounts to maintain access.
techniques.agent_relevance: Agent issuing iam:CreateAccessKey to bootstrap a follow-on identity.

techniques.name: Masquerading
techniques.description: Adversaries manipulate features of artifacts to look legitimate.
